purpose: Capture post-foundation implementation gaps and follow-up work without mixing in Enterprise or KRITIS scope.
goal: Convert cleanup gaps into a machine-checkable task track with explicit status rollups and end summaries.

items.title: Wire domain action router into production task execution path
items.notes: Current router is foundation skeleton; production execution integration is still pending.

items.title: Extend domain audit with CI artifact publishing
items.notes: Publish domain audit report in CI artifacts for release evidence review.

items.title: Harden domain retrieval tests with retrieval backend contract fixtures
items.notes: Add integration-like fixtures for source-type routing and bounded chunk selection.

items.title: Implement Blender addon runtime skeleton
items.notes: Deliver addon bootstrap and bridge runtime files before any runtime_mvp claim.

items.title: Add Blender smoke runner and runtime evidence capture
items.notes: Required for promoting Blender from foundation_only to runtime_mvp.

items.title: Align Blender task IDs to generic service ownership map
items.notes: Keep blender-specific tasks from reintroducing duplicated registry/policy/retrieval logic.

items.title: Prepare FreeCAD domain descriptor baseline
items.notes: Descriptor/capability groundwork only; no runtime execution in this cleanup scope.

items.title: Prepare KiCad domain descriptor baseline
items.notes: Descriptor/capability groundwork only; no runtime execution in this cleanup scope.

tasks.title: Wire domain action router into production task execution path
acceptance_criteria: Domain action router is reachable in the production task execution flow.
Execution-like domain actions stay behind hub policy/approval gatekeeping.
No direct client-side or worker-local bypass path is introduced.

tasks.title: Extend domain audit with CI artifact publishing
acceptance_criteria: Domain audit output can be produced as CI artifact evidence.
Report path and format are documented and reproducible.
Failure states are visible in CI logs and artifact metadata.

tasks.title: Harden domain retrieval tests with retrieval backend contract fixtures
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval tests use backend contract fixtures for representative source types.
Chunk selection remains bounded and deterministic for the tested paths.
Fixture coverage catches regressions in retrieval routing behavior.

tasks.title: Implement Blender addon runtime skeleton
acceptance_criteria: Runtime skeleton files exist for the Blender addon execution bridge.
Skeleton stays additive to existing hub-worker orchestration boundaries.
No runtime_mvp claim is made before execution evidence exists.

tasks.title: Add Blender smoke runner and runtime evidence capture
acceptance_criteria: A Blender smoke runner verifies minimal runtime wiring end-to-end.
Runtime evidence is captured in an auditable artifact/report.
Promotion criteria from foundation_only to runtime_mvp are explicit.

tasks.title: Align Blender task IDs to generic service ownership map
acceptance_criteria: Blender task IDs map cleanly to generic ownership boundaries.
Duplicated registry/policy/retrieval ownership claims are removed.
Alignment is documented for future Blender tasks.

tasks.title: Prepare FreeCAD domain descriptor baseline
acceptance_criteria: Descriptor baseline for FreeCAD is prepared in deferred scope.
No runtime execution integration is introduced in this step.
Output remains compatible with generic domain descriptor contracts.

tasks.title: Prepare KiCad domain descriptor baseline
acceptance_criteria: Descriptor baseline for KiCad is prepared in deferred scope.
No runtime execution integration is introduced in this step.
Output remains compatible with generic domain descriptor contracts.

notes: Ausgangsdatei enthielt cleanup_buckets, aber keine tasks_status_summary/critical_path/milestone-Rollups und war deshalb mit dem Konsistenzvalidator nicht kompatibel.
cleanup_buckets wurden als fachliche Quelle beibehalten; tasks/milestones/critical_path und Rollups wurden daraus abgeleitet.
Risikoeinstufung wurde konsistent aus Prioritaeten abgeleitet (P0->critical, P1->high, P2->medium, P3->low).
End-Summaries wurden als Abschlussblock fuer menschliche Nachvollziehbarkeit ergänzt.

summary_notes: Diese Datei bleibt OSS-cleanup-orientiert und erweitert keine KRITIS/Enterprise-Scope-Anforderungen.
Der Track ist jetzt sowohl menschenlesbar (cleanup_buckets) als auch maschinenlesbar (tasks_status_summary/execution_stage_summary).
Die task- und milestone-Rollups sind synchronisiert und validator-kompatibel.
scope_guardrails: No Enterprise-only RBAC, tenancy or KRITIS requirements are included in this cleanup todo.
Cleanup remains OSS-foundation focused and additive to the existing hub-governed architecture.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die bisherige Datei war inhaltlich brauchbar, aber strukturell unvollständig für bestehende Track-Konsistenzprüfungen.
Es fehlten insbesondere Tasks-Rollups, kritischer Pfad und Milestone-Zusammenfassung.

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: cleanup_buckets wurden in ein konsistentes tasks/milestones-Modell überführt, ohne den ursprünglichen Inhalt zu verlieren.
tasks_status_summary, critical_path und milestone-Counter wurden ergänzt und synchronisiert.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen/Erweiterungen
items: analysis_checks und summary_notes ergänzt, um Korrekturen und Annahmen transparent zu dokumentieren.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Aktueller Umsetzungsstand: 8 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 0 done (0.0%).
Kritischer Pfad enthält 3 P0-Aufgaben (0 erledigt, 3 offen).
